title: Did Evangelion change your standards taste on other anime
display_title: Did Evangelion change your standards taste on other anime
wikitext:
[]: Did Evangelion change your standards/taste on other anime?             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Sun Oct 13, 2013 8:46 pm I think this goes here since its more of an eva question than a general anime question since the off-topic board is suppose to be offtopic. I heard there was a few people who love Eva and hate Gurren Lagann because it was the anti-eva. Just curious to see if something like that applied to most people here to some extent. This could apply to other medium like TV and such. I noticed some live action tv shows having very lame or hammy acting that I never noticed before after watching the series again. This could just be a coincidence or maybe its because I watched too much animation recently.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Oct 13, 2013 9:56 pm Personally, I'm still very much able to enjoy something like Bleach or Naruto. I think that being a pedantic hipster is a bad thing - Art comes in many forms, and closing yourself off to some will just make you miss things - that doesn't mean that you have to have active interest in everything , that's not even possible. But there's deep stuff, and there's fun stuff, and both has its merits, and its times.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Oct 14, 2013 12:21 am Just becoming oversaturated with anime changed my tastes: I learned that most anime is derivative garbage, and instead of watching crap just because it was anime, I started holding out for the very best. NGE is just that one thing that speaks so much to me personally, it's not going to get topped by anything anytime soon.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Oct 14, 2013 12:41 am I now expect anime to get to a point of some kind. I used to be an InuYasha fan, but I shall pine for a kiss no more forever.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Oct 14, 2013 1:22 am Ever since watching Eva I've increasingly shied away from darker, more "introspective" works for fear of having a similar reaction to them as I did Eva (weeks long depressions, existential crises, irrational attachments to characters, etc). Combined with the sheer joy I had watching Star Driver that fall, and later Horizon seasons 1 and 2, I've pretty much decided I'm going to stick with fun shows that are fun, and leave the darker stories to people who aren't as prone to dramatic downward mood swings as me. That doesn't mean I won't watch them, but I won't seek them out either.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 14, 2013 1:57 am I got into Japanese media via the '80/'90s boom in manga translations, so I'd pretty much decided that there were way too many sex-change comedies, silly mechs and overlong romances; and that the only good stuff was the girls+guns sci-fi. When, a decade ago, I picked up some anime DVDs, that was what I went for in the first place (things like BurnUp and Dirty Pair Flash ); but as NGE had a reputation of "if you watch only one super-robot series, watch this one", and the early episodes of that were on the shelf too, I tried it. If it had any lasting effect in terms of choices, it was to broaden the range of anime I would consider -- aided by the fact that it later became possible to enjoy anime at less than the £4-5/episode rate that DVDs of a decade ago involved.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Oct 14, 2013 2:04 am I watched NGE and ended up loving it after hearing from numerous sources that it is "overrated" and "pretentious". If anything, that experience taught me to be more open when choosing anime to watch.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Mon Oct 14, 2013 2:15 am Reichu wrote: Just becoming oversaturated with anime changed my tastes: I learned that most anime is derivative garbage, and instead of watching crap just because it was anime, I started holding out for the very best.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 14, 2013 2:30 am I'm not a big watcher of anime(the number of series I've seen could be counted on my fingers), so about the narrative and what I want for a plot it didn't changed many things, the fact I'm very eclectic in what I see helping a lot(I can go from FMA to Panty&Stocking to NGE to Nichijou to Black Lagoon to Kill me Baby! , with a side-step toward Avatar the last airbender and Legend of Korra to recently Attack on Titan ) What it did changed are my standards on the animation and graphics quality : after seeing the huge slap in the face that were NGE and especially the Rebuild, I find myself finding many others animes lacking on the animation and immediately picks the moment were 3-D CGI are used, especially prevalent in Attack on Titan , although for this one it's compensated by the sheer badassness of said scenes!(the 3DMG and full use, the chase in the forest of Giant Trees! )

[]:             Quote Post by Black Swordsman » Mon Oct 14, 2013 7:26 am Well I've only watched Gundam 00, Gundam Unicorn, Gundam SEED/Destiny, Gundam 08th MS Team, Gundam AGE, Attack On Titan and Sword Art Online on the anime front (aside from Evangelion) but I still enjoy all of them just as much as I did before. However, right after watching Evangelion I struggle to watch anything else as nothing really compares But a few days later and I manage!

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Oct 14, 2013 7:35 am Eva didn't change my view of anime - it created it, because it was the first anime I saw. It drew me in, and although I've seen a lot more since, the desire to have anime try to put over some kind of message hasn't gone away.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Oct 14, 2013 7:54 am It alerted me that it was possible to have an auteur viewpoint in TV animation, but I wouldn't say it was the anime that made me change my opinion on anime. I already made the decision that anime was worth getting involved with years before Evangelion, and if anything, it made me start looking into Anno more as a director more and look into the production of an anime a lot more seriously than I already did.

[]: Re: Did Evangelion change your standards/taste on other anim             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 8:46 am NGE was the only anime I've ever watched, start to finish (studio ghibli films notwithstanding). I consider it not only an anime but a work - similar to a musical Opus. NGE informed me that it was possible for great entertainment and story having actual meaning to be expressed in any medium. In fact, one need not look further than the music accompaniment in NGE compared to that employed in some of the lighter fare (ie., classical music vs sappy J-pop) to see the difference.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Oct 14, 2013 10:57 am The first anime I saw was AKIRA . The second anime I saw was Gunslinger Girl, Season 1 . The third anime I saw was the first half of Nadia: The Secret of Blue Water . Getting bored with the infamous Island Arc, I supplemented it with Neon Genesis Evangelion , hoping to still get some Anno goodness during his temporary departure from Nadia episodes, and finished both shows roughly at the same time. Some Miyazaki films may have been sprinkled here and there in between those shows. So, no. NGE didn't change my standards. But my standards were pretty darned high to begin with. (Which is one of the reasons why I held out introducing myself to anime until I was in film school.)

[]: Re: Did Evangelion change your standards/taste on other anim             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 14, 2013 12:56 pm monitoradiation wrote: In fact, one need not look further than the music accompaniment in NGE compared to that employed in some of the lighter fare (ie., classical music vs sappy J-pop) to see the difference.

[]: Re: Did Evangelion change your standards/taste on other anim             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Oct 14, 2013 1:08 pm And RahXephon had its bit of Borodin. monitoradiation wrote: classical music vs sappy J-pop

[]:             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Mon Oct 14, 2013 2:17 pm Did my taste change after NGE? Let me see: Death Note(2010)- no change NGE(2010)- no change Kara no Kyoukai(2011)- small change Serial Experiments Lain(2011/2012)- small change Nope, it started with KnK and Serial Experiments Lain, it seems.

[]: Re: Did Evangelion change your standards/taste on other anim             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Mon Oct 14, 2013 3:57 pm Mr. Tines wrote: I wouldn't use that as a diagnostic. NGE is by far from the only series to draw heavily on Western Classical music. There's series like Princess Tutu or Nodame Cantabile , where it's bound up with the story; but it's used in such unlikely series as Letter Bee , which, while fun, was hardly on the same level (and I hear rumours that Mars of Destruction featured a gratuitous burst of Beethoven).

[]:             Quote Post by LegionWrex » Tue Oct 15, 2013 3:49 pm NGE got me into what I like to call the "mindfuck" anime. These types of animes like to play with your head and mess you up. Stuff like Serial Experiments Lain, Revolutionary Girl Ultema, Mirai Nikki and, to an extent, Welcome to the NHK. Still, I enjoy "fun" anime a lot, most notably FMA, FMA:B, Bleach and Gundam.

[]:             Quote Post by Madonna » Thu Oct 17, 2013 8:19 pm I don't think it really changed anything, to this day I hold the view point that any artistic medium whether it be literature, film, music etc can be used to make derivative superficial nonsense (not that I don't enjoy mindless entertainment from time to time, mind you) or something with more depth . It does lead into an interesting discussion of where Evangelion falls in the spectrum of anime, I know some who consider it to be pretentious nonsense and frivolous; others consider it a masterpiece. I wouldn't label it as either, it surely has it's technical flaws, but I enjoy the plot and love the characters too much to ever consider it superficial. I would say this, although I don't consider Evangelion to be the golden standard in anime, it personally resonates with me far more than many others due to my personal connection to it.

Article: Did Evangelion change your standards taste on other anime
 Oct 17, 2013 8:19 pm I don't think it really changed anything, to this day I hold the view point that any artistic medium whether it be literature, film, music etc can be used to make derivative superficial nonsense (not that I don't enjoy mindless entertainment from time to time, mind you) or something with more depth . It does lead into an interesting discussion of where Evangelion falls in the spectrum of anime, I know some who consider it to be pretentious nonsense and frivolous; others consider it a masterpiece. I wouldn't label it as either, it surely has it's technical flaws, but I enjoy the plot and love the characters too much to ever consider it superficial. I would say this, although I don't consider Evangelion to be the golden standard in anime, it personally resonates with me far more than many others due to my personal connection to it.
